transcript_evidence.topic: Helping young people (Gen Z) stay motivated and thrive at work
transcript_evidence.interviewer_question: [41.4-49.5s] '...what have we got to know about them and do to make sure that they thrive, stay motivated and achieve their goals?'
transcript_evidence.core_argument: [71.8-82.6s] 'I don't like the conversations of strengths and weaknesses because strengths often have liability... in the wrong context you're arrogant.'
transcript_evidence.weakness_example: [83.7-89.9s] 'weaknesses also have silver linings. So for me, I'm chronically disorganized.'
transcript_evidence.story: [102.7-131.8s] Networking event anecdote: lost a business card, could not follow up, found it two weeks later.
transcript_evidence.repeated_phrase_removed: [131.9-135.3s] 'What this important piece of business would have led to because I lost the card.' — exact rephrasing of 127.9-131.3s.
transcript_evidence.punchline: [139.1-147.1s] 'He wanted to work with me more because he thought I was busy.'
transcript_evidence.conclusion: [148.7-160.5s] 'So strength or weakness, the answer is it depends... but not always is the point.'

removed_defects.reason: Interviewer speaking; context established from 41.4s only

False starts: 'This is my own bias. I don't like the conversation. My own bias. I don't like the conversation of strengths and weaknesses.' — repeated twice before the complete articulation begins at 71.8s; also dead air gaps at 50-53s and 55-66s
Long dead air (90-96s, 99-102s) plus redundant rumination 'Every system app works for a week then I'm back... for years I used to beat myself up'
Internal pause (0.8s) removed within networking-story segment
Phrase loop: 'What this important piece of business would have led to because I lost the card.' is a near-verbatim repeat of the preceding sentence
Gen-Z section starts mid-thought and ends with an incomplete sentence at 211s (video cuts off); keeping only the self-contained preface argument preserves meaning

operations.reason: Interviewer question sets up the Gen Z / thriving at work topic
Core premise: don't use strength/weakness labels — context determines outcome
Weaknesses also have silver linings — introduces personal disorganization example
Story setup: disorganization framed as context-dependent weakness
Networking event story: met impressed executive, given business card
Lost the card — contrast with organized person who would have followed up
Two weeks later found the card (resuming after removing repeated phrase)
Punchline: emailed him; he wanted to work together MORE thinking speaker was busy
Conclusion: strength or weakness depends on context; it IS a liability in general
Coda: 'but not always is the point' — closes the self-contained argument
Normalize spoken audio to EBU R128 target for comfortable listening

parameters.method: openai-whisper base model word-level timestamps mapped to output timeline
parameters.caption_style: white text, black outline (Outline=2, Shadow=1), FontSize=28, center-bottom (Alignment=2, MarginV=30)
parameters.grouping: max 6 words per caption, max 3.0s per caption, break at segment boundaries and pauses >0.8s

operations.reason: Burned-in captions for accessibility; SRT saved separately

subtitle_generation_method: openai-whisper 'base' model transcribed /workspace/work/audio.wav; word-level timestamps from Whisper segments mapped through source→output segment timeline; grouped into 54 caption chunks respecting segment boundaries
caption_style: ASS subtitles: FontSize=28, PrimaryColour=white (#FFFFFF), OutlineColour=black (#000000), BorderStyle=1, Outline=2, Shadow=1, Alignment=2 (center-bottom), MarginV=30px
checks_performed: ffprobe materials/source.mp4 — confirmed 211.1s, 1280x720, H.264, AAC stereo 48kHz
librosa RMS analysis — identified 33 silence regions >0.5s and 5 major section breaks (frozen frames at 36, 37, 66, 102, 157s in source)
Whisper ASR transcription — identified false start loop at 51-56s, phrase loop at 131.9-135.3s, dead air at 89.9-102.7s
Clip extraction with -t (duration) and setpts=PTS-STARTPTS — verified correct duration per clip
Concat + loudnorm two-pass — verified joined duration 73.33s
ffprobe submit/output.mp4 — duration=73.54s (within 60-75s target), H.264 1280x720 30fps, AAC 48kHz stereo
Frame sampling at 2fps — detected 0 black frames, 1 natural pause frame (not frozen content, speaker pausing at 59s output = 144s source)
SRT file: 54 captions, 216 lines, covering full output duration
post_first_export_changes: Fixed B-frame PTS issue: replaced -to with -t (duration) and added setpts=PTS-STARTPTS for accurate clip extraction — clip 09 was producing 16.8s video (2x expected) due to B-frame timestamp confusion with -to flag
Regenerated captions with segment-aware grouping to prevent words from different source segments merging into same caption line
